channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Projecting the 49ers’ starting lineup in the wake of roster overhaul
item.description: Let's pencil out the 49ers' starting lineup after this week's overhaul of the roster amid NFL free agency.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Bay Area News Group boys athlete of the week: Luke Dafferner, Acalanes lacrosse
item.description: In the boys poll, Dafferner had 40.78% of the vote to finish first. Mountain View soccer player Aidan Wong was second at 31.07%, and Priory basketball player Kasten Eggers took third at 19.49%.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Teen track runner who bashed opponent's head rallies supporters in Virginia after getting assault charge
item.description: Virginia teen Alaila Everett held a rally outside of her high school Thursday, as supporters showed sympathy amid national backlash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shohei Ohtani and four other Japanese players come home to start the MLB season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — It's only two games to start the Major League Baseball season — the World Series-champion Dodgers against the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday and Wednesday at the Tokyo Dome. For the record, the MLB regular season consists of almost 2,500 games.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WADE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heartbreak for DePaul as 1st trip to Big East semifinals slips away with late collapse at MSG
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At long last, downtrodden DePaul was finally about to produce its own indelible moment at the Big East Tournament. A big upset was in the bag for the Blue Demons — and their first trip to the semifinals at Madison Square Garden. And then it slipped through their fingers.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lakers' James, Hachimura, Hayes fly back to California for treatment and will miss rest of road trip
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — LeBron James flew back to California for treatment on his left groin strain and will miss the rest of the Los Angeles Lakers’ four-game trip, which concludes Friday at Denver.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sean McVay's custom highlight tapes of Davante Adams helped lure the WR to Rams
item.description: Sean McVay has long eyed bringing Davante Adams to Los Angeles, so when Adams became available this offseason, the Rams coach didn't let him slip away.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Camilo Villegas and Lucas Glover among those sharing Players Championship lead on wild day
item.description: PONTE VEDRA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Camilo Villegas was on the phone the day before The Players Championship trying to sort out his swing with a coach who is in Singapore caddying at a LIV Golf event. He wound up tied for the lead, a peculiar twist in a tournament filled with them.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Diego FC announces measures to discourage fans from engaging in homophobic chant
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — San Diego FC will use pre-match and in-stadium messaging, enhanced security measures and strong enforcement of policies to discourage fans from engaging in what it called “discriminatory chants” at home matches, the team announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Diego soccer team enhances security to eject fans who use common Mexican 'anti-gay' chant during game
item.description: San Diego FC is making significant additions to its security and stadium operations to curb fans from using a common Mexican chant during games.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Duke star Cooper Flagg leaves ACC tournament in wheelchair after suffering injury in win over Georgia Tech
item.description: Duke star freshman Cooper Flagg left the team's win over Georgia Tech in the first half after suffering an apparent ankle injury grabbing a rebound.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays withdraw from planned $1.3 billion ballpark in St. Petersburg, citing storms, delays
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The Tampa Bay Rays withdrew Thursday from a $1.3 billion project to construct a new ballpark adjacent to Tropicana Field, citing a hurricane and delays that likely drove up the proposal's cost.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry becomes investor in Unrivaled Basketball
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Stephen Curry has joined Unrivaled basketball as an investor. The Golden State Warriors guard is the latest sports star to join the 3v3 basketball league as an investor, joining the likes of the NBA's Giannis Antetokounmpo and Carmelo Anthony, and tennis star Coco Gauff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Josh Giddey scores season-high 29 points before injuring ankle and Bulls beat Pacers 121-103
item.description: Josh Giddey had a season-high 29 points along with 10 rebounds before leaving midway through the fourth quarter with a right ankle injury, and the Chicago Bulls won their third straight game, 121-103 over the Indiana Pacers on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cavaliers rely on scoring balance to beat Bucks 112-100 for 14th consecutive victory
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Max Strus had 17 points to lead a balanced scoring attack as the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Milwaukee Bucks 112-100 on Sunday night for their 14th consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nearing the end of a short spring, Cubs and Dodgers have made final preparations for season openers in Japan
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Shohei Ohtani's bat is ready for the defending World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers, while Shota Imanaga has looked good on the mound for the Chicago Cubs with less than a week remaining before baseball's regular-season opener in Japan.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history at $40 million per year
item.description: Myles Garrett is staying in Cleveland after the Browns gave him a record four-year contract extension that makes the four-time All-Pro edge rusher the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy released after Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
